items.briefing:
Google has warned that cyber attackers are handing off access to compromised systems in as little as 22 seconds — meaning even a brief delay in responding to an alert can be too late, particularly for public sector organisations. This is a timely reminder for all Australian staff that quick reporting of suspicious activity is critical. Separately, Australia is progressing its framework for account-to-account payments, which will bring new digital payment options but also potential new avenues for fraud.

**Practical tip:** If you receive a suspicious email or notice something unusual on your device, report it to your IT team immediately — don't wait until later in the day.

featured.title: Google warns public sector on 22-second cyber hand-off
featured.quote: Australian public sector faces critical ransomware vulnerability with 22-second cyber attack window.
